title: Makers / Working Artists
thesis: Culture in western Nevada County is something people make, teach, share, and practice together.
dek: A working-arts path that follows art from a public gallery into studios, classes, tools, and hands-on creative practice.
copy: This path shows that the Discovery Map can point to culture being made, taught, shared, and practiced, not only culture on a calendar. Art Works Gallery, ASiF Studios, and The Curious Forge each make the creative process visible: artists showing current work, studio communities teaching and practicing skills, and maker spaces opening tools and craft to more people.

stops.name: Art Works Gallery
stops.note: Start with working artists presenting finished pieces in a cooperative downtown gallery.

stops.name: ASiF Studios
stops.note: Continue into a studio community where classes and shared workspace keep art practice visible.

stops.name: The Curious Forge
stops.note: End at a maker space where tools, workshops, and hands-on learning broaden what creative production can mean.

title: Pond, Ridge & Schoolhouse
thesis: The quiet side of Nevada County culture — a pond trail in town and a century-old schoolhouse on the San Juan Ridge.
dek: A slower path: walk Hirschman's Pond at the edge of Nevada City, then drive the Ridge to the North Columbia Schoolhouse.
copy: Not everything cultural happens on a stage. This pairing puts the landscape itself on the itinerary — a wetland trail reclaimed from hydraulic mining, and a one-room schoolhouse that became the Ridge's gathering place for music, film, and storytelling.

stops.name: Hirschman Trail / Hirschman's Pond
stops.note: A flat wetland loop minutes from downtown — herons, ponds, and the scars of hydraulic mining gone green.

stops.name: North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center
stops.note: An 1875 one-room schoolhouse, now the Ridge's living room for concerts, films, and storytelling festivals.
